label: Ae Accountability
description: The extent to which the statement establishes mechanisms for holding AI developers, deployers, or operators responsible for harms or failures. Strong coverage includes: specifies liability frameworks, audit requirements, or accountability chains; names responsible parties; mandates post-deployment monitoring or incident reporting obligations.

label: Ae Autonomy Preservation
description: The extent to which the statement protects human agency, self-determination, and independent decision-making from erosion by AI systems. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses AI-driven manipulation, nudging, or choice architecture; mandates protections against autonomy-undermining personalization; prohibits subliminal or coercive AI influence.

label: Ae Beneficence
description: The extent to which the statement orients AI development toward human flourishing, the common good, or broad societal benefit. Strong coverage includes: centers AI development around measurable social outcomes (health, education, poverty reduction); mandates public benefit assessments; explicitly subordinates commercial interests to public good.

label: Ae Contestability Redress
description: The extent to which the statement establishes rights to challenge AI decisions and obtain meaningful remedy for harms. Strong coverage includes: mandates appeal mechanisms for automated decisions; specifies timelines and procedures for contestation; requires human review upon challenge; establishes compensation or restitution frameworks.

label: Ae Corrigibility
description: The extent to which the statement demands that advanced AI systems remain correctable, interruptible, and redirectable by human operators. Strong coverage includes: explicitly requires kill switches, shutdown mechanisms, or alignment guarantees; mandates that AI systems cannot resist correction or develop self-preservation goals; references corrigibility by name.

label: Ae Data Sovereignty
description: The extent to which the statement asserts control over personal, community, or national data, including data localization requirements and resistance to cross-border data extraction. Strong coverage includes: demands data localization, community data trusts, or indigenous data sovereignty (e.g., CARE principles); opposes cross-border data extraction without consent; specifies data governance architectures.

label: Ae Dual Use Prevention
description: The extent to which the statement addresses prevention of AI misuse for harmful purposes, including weaponization, surveillance, and disinformation. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific dual-use risks (bioweapon design, autonomous targeting, mass surveillance); mandates use restrictions, export controls, or know-your-customer requirements for AI capabilities.

label: Ae Environmental Sustainability
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the environmental footprint of AI systems, including energy consumption, carbon emissions, and resource extraction. Strong coverage includes: mandates carbon accounting for AI training and inference; requires energy efficiency standards or renewable energy use for data centers; addresses water use, mineral extraction, or e-waste from AI hardware.

label: Ae Epistemic Honesty
description: The extent to which the statement demands truthful, non-misleading communication about AI capabilities, limitations, uncertainties, and known failure modes. Strong coverage includes: explicitly prohibits overstatement of AI capabilities; requires disclosure of known limitations, error rates, or confidence intervals; mandates honesty about what AI cannot do.

label: Ae Fairness
description: The extent to which the statement addresses non-discrimination, bias mitigation, and equitable treatment in AI systems. Strong coverage includes: mandates bias audits, fairness metrics, or disparate impact assessments; specifies protected categories; requires pre-deployment testing for discriminatory outcomes.

label: Ae Human Dignity
description: The extent to which the statement invokes respect for inherent human worth, resisting the reduction of persons to data points or optimization targets. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes human dignity as a foundational constraint on AI; prohibits AI uses that degrade, dehumanize, or instrumentalize persons; references dignity in constitutional or human rights frameworks.

label: Ae Human Oversight Operational
description: The extent to which the statement requires human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop oversight for AI operations, particularly in high-stakes decisions. Strong coverage includes: mandates human review before consequential AI decisions (sentencing, hiring, medical diagnosis); specifies human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop requirements; defines domains where full automation is prohibited.

label: Ae Inclusivity Accessibility
description: The extent to which the statement ensures AI benefits, tools, and governance processes are accessible to all populations including marginalized and underrepresented groups. Strong coverage includes: mandates accessibility standards (WCAG) for AI interfaces; requires multilingual support; addresses digital divides; mandates inclusive design processes involving marginalized communities.

label: Ae Informed Consent
description: The extent to which the statement requires meaningful, informed consent from individuals or communities before AI data collection, processing, or automated decision-making. Strong coverage includes: mandates explicit, informed, and revocable consent for data use in AI training or deployment; specifies consent must be granular, purpose-specific, and freely given; addresses power asymmetries in consent.

label: Ae Privacy
description: The extent to which the statement addresses data protection, privacy preservation, and limits on data collection in AI systems. Strong coverage includes: invokes specific data protection frameworks (GDPR, CCPA); mandates privacy-by-design, data minimization, or differential privacy; requires data protection impact assessments.

label: Ae Purpose Limitation
description: The extent to which the statement restricts AI systems and data use to their stated purposes, opposing function creep or secondary repurposing. Strong coverage includes: explicitly prohibits repurposing of data or AI systems beyond original stated purpose; mandates purpose specification and technical enforcement of use restrictions; addresses function creep.

label: Ae Robustness Reliability
description: The extent to which the statement demands that AI systems perform reliably, resist adversarial attacks, and degrade gracefully under unexpected conditions. Strong coverage includes: mandates adversarial robustness testing, formal verification, or reliability certification; specifies performance standards under distribution shift; requires fail-safe or graceful degradation mechanisms.

label: Ae Safety Catastrophic
description: The extent to which the statement addresses prevention of large-scale, civilizational, or existential risks from advanced AI systems. Strong coverage includes: explicitly references existential risk, loss of human control over superintelligent AI, or civilizational-scale catastrophe; calls for moratoriums, international treaties, or compute governance to prevent catastrophic outcomes.

label: Ae Safety Near Term
description: The extent to which the statement addresses prevention of concrete, immediate, and foreseeable harms from current AI systems. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific near-term harms (deepfakes, autonomous weapons misuse, algorithmic discrimination); mandates safety testing, red-teaming, or pre-deployment risk assessments; specifies harm thresholds.

label: Ae Security Cybersecurity
description: The extent to which the statement addresses protection of AI systems from cyberattacks, data breaches, model theft, and adversarial exploitation. Strong coverage includes: mandates cybersecurity standards for AI systems; addresses model poisoning, prompt injection, or data exfiltration; requires security audits or certification; references specific threat models.

label: Ae Transparency
description: The extent to which the statement calls for AI systems to be transparent about their workings, decision logic, and outputs. Strong coverage includes: explicitly demands algorithmic transparency or explainability; invokes right to explanation; mandates disclosure requirements for model architecture, training data, or decision criteria.

label: Dg Anti Corruption
description: The extent to which the statement addresses corruption risks in AI procurement, governance, and the revolving door between tech companies and regulators. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific corruption vectors (AI procurement fraud, revolving door, regulatory capture, lobbying); mandates conflict-of-interest disclosures, cooling-off periods, or procurement integrity standards.

label: Dg Checks And Balances
description: The extent to which the statement demands constitutional or institutional checks on concentrations of AI-related power, whether governmental or corporate. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific concentrations of AI power (big tech, intelligence agencies, executive authority) and demands structural countervailing mechanisms; proposes new institutional checks for the AI era.

label: Dg Civil Society Participation
description: The extent to which the statement demands meaningful roles for civil society organizations, NGOs, and community groups in AI governance. Strong coverage includes: mandates civil society seats on AI governance bodies; requires public comment periods for AI regulations; funds civil society capacity for AI oversight; establishes participatory governance structures.

label: Dg Democratic Governance
description: The extent to which the statement demands democratic control, legitimacy, and popular mandate for AI policy decisions. Strong coverage includes: demands that AI policy be subject to democratic processes (legislative debate, referenda, citizens' assemblies); opposes technocratic or corporate capture of AI governance; requires elected-body oversight.

label: Dg Electoral Integrity
description: The extent to which the statement addresses protection of elections from AI-enabled manipulation, including deepfakes, micro-targeting, and computational propaganda. Strong coverage includes: demands regulation of AI in political campaigns; mandates labeling of AI-generated political content; proposes restrictions on algorithmic micro-targeting during elections; addresses foreign AI-enabled election interference.

label: Dg Freedom Of Press
description: The extent to which the statement addresses press freedom in covering AI issues, including access to information, whistleblower protection, and resistance to AI-enabled media manipulation. Strong coverage includes: demands press access to AI system information for investigative journalism; protects whistleblowers who expose AI harms; addresses AI-generated disinformation as a threat to press integrity.

label: Dg Government Accountability
description: The extent to which the statement demands that governments be accountable for their own AI deployments in public services, law enforcement, and administration. Strong coverage includes: mandates government AI registries, public audits of government AI systems, or algorithmic impact assessments for public-sector AI; requires legislative reporting on government AI use.

label: Dg Independent Oversight
description: The extent to which the statement calls for independent regulatory agencies, ombudspersons, or audit bodies for AI oversight. Strong coverage includes: calls for independent AI regulatory agencies with statutory authority, funding, and enforcement power; specifies independence criteria (budget autonomy, appointment procedures); references existing models (data protection authorities).

label: Dg Informed Citizenry
description: The extent to which the statement emphasizes public understanding of AI capabilities, risks, and governance as essential for democratic self-governance. Strong coverage includes: mandates public AI literacy programs; funds independent AI journalism and research; requires plain-language explanations of AI policies; addresses AI misinformation as a democratic threat.

label: Dg Legitimacy Of Governance
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the legitimacy conditions for AI governance institutions, including representativeness, procedural fairness, and accountability. Strong coverage includes: explicitly analyzes legitimacy deficits in current AI governance (unrepresentative, unaccountable, dominated by Global North or industry); proposes legitimacy criteria for AI governance bodies.

label: Dg Liberal Democratic Norms
description: The extent to which the statement frames liberal democratic values (individual rights, pluralism, constitutionalism) as the normative foundation for AI governance. Strong coverage includes: explicitly positions liberal democratic values as the governing framework for AI; opposes authoritarian AI governance models; frames AI governance as an extension of constitutional democracy.

label: Dg Open Government
description: The extent to which the statement demands transparency and openness in government AI decisions, procurement, and policy-making. Strong coverage includes: mandates open-source government AI systems, public algorithmic impact assessments, or FOIA applicability to government AI; requires public registers of government AI use cases.

label: Dg Public Deliberation
description: The extent to which the statement calls for structured public deliberation on AI policy, including citizens' assemblies, public consultations, and deliberative processes. Strong coverage includes: calls for citizens' assemblies, deliberative polls, or structured public consultations on AI policy; references specific deliberative methods; mandates public input before major AI deployments.

label: Dg Rule Of Law
description: The extent to which the statement demands that AI governance be subject to legal frameworks, statutory authority, and judicial review rather than industry self-regulation alone. Strong coverage includes: explicitly calls for binding AI legislation, statutory regulatory authority, or treaty-based governance; opposes purely voluntary codes; demands judicial reviewability of AI-related decisions.

label: Dg Separation Of Powers
description: The extent to which the statement addresses institutional checks on AI governance power, preventing concentration of AI authority in any single branch or entity. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses separation of powers in AI governance; demands that regulatory, enforcement, and adjudicative functions be distributed; opposes executive overreach in AI policy; requires legislative authorization.

label: Dg Subsidiarity
description: The extent to which the statement advocates for AI governance decisions to be made at the most local appropriate level, resisting unnecessary centralization. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes subsidiarity; demands local or regional AI governance authority; opposes top-down AI policy that ignores local contexts; advocates for community-level AI governance.

label: Ec Ai Capacity Building
description: The extent to which the statement prioritizes workforce development, AI training programs, and building AI human capital as a strategic priority. Strong coverage includes: central focus on AI workforce development; proposes national AI training programs, university curricula, or reskilling initiatives as strategic priorities; sets targets for AI talent pipeline.

label: Ec Ai Financialization
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the financialization of AI, including speculative investment, valuation bubbles, and the distortion of AI development by financial incentives. Strong coverage includes: critiques speculative AI investment, hype cycles, or misaligned financial incentives driving unsafe AI development; proposes guardrails on AI-related financial speculation; addresses venture capital influence on AI safety trade-offs.

label: Ec Ai For Development
description: The extent to which the statement frames AI primarily as a tool for achieving national development goals, rather than as an object of governance. Strong coverage includes: frames AI predominantly as an instrument for economic development, poverty reduction, or SDG achievement; subordinates governance concerns to developmental outcomes; treats AI adoption as development strategy.

label: Ec Algorithmic Management
description: The extent to which the statement addresses AI-driven workplace surveillance, performance monitoring, scheduling, and management of workers. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific algorithmic management harms (constant surveillance, automated firing, pace-setting, emotional monitoring); demands transparency in workplace algorithms; mandates worker access to their own performance data.

label: Ec Anti Monopoly
description: The extent to which the statement opposes monopolistic concentration of AI capabilities, data, compute, or market power in a small number of corporations. Strong coverage includes: names specific concentration risks (Big Tech dominance, compute moats, data monopolies); demands structural separation, acquisition limits, or mandatory licensing; proposes public alternatives to concentrated private AI.

label: Ec Collective Bargaining
description: The extent to which the statement supports worker collective bargaining rights, unionization, and worker voice in AI-related workplace decisions. Strong coverage includes: mandates collective bargaining over AI deployment in workplaces; supports worker right to organize around AI issues; requires worker consent before algorithmic management implementation; references ILO conventions.

label: Ec Data Rents Enclosure
description: The extent to which the statement critiques data rent-seeking, data enclosure, and the appropriation of collectively-produced data by private actors. Strong coverage includes: explicitly critiques data enclosure or data rent-seeking; proposes data commons, data trusts, or data dividends; frames personal/community data as a commons rather than a corporate asset; references Zuboff, Morozov, or political economy of data.

label: Ec Digital Infrastructure Access
description: The extent to which the statement addresses access to the digital infrastructure (broadband, compute, cloud, devices) necessary to participate in the AI economy. Strong coverage includes: demands universal broadband, public compute infrastructure, or subsidized access to cloud/AI services; frames digital infrastructure as essential public utility; addresses infrastructure gaps between urban/rural, rich/poor, North/South.

label: Ec Economic Security Ubi
description: The extent to which the statement proposes economic safety nets, universal basic income, or other income guarantees in response to AI-driven economic disruption. Strong coverage includes: proposes UBI, universal basic services, or other unconditional income guarantees funded by AI-related revenues; frames economic security as a right in the AI era; specifies funding mechanisms.

label: Ec Equitable Ai Benefits
description: The extent to which the statement demands fair distribution of AI-generated wealth, productivity gains, and social benefits. Strong coverage includes: proposes specific redistribution mechanisms (profit-sharing, public AI dividends, community benefit agreements); demands that productivity gains be shared with workers and communities; frames AI benefits as a commons.

label: Ec Fair Taxation Of Ai
description: The extent to which the statement addresses taxation of AI profits, automation, or data extraction to fund public goods. Strong coverage includes: proposes specific AI-related taxes (robot tax, automation tax, data tax, windfall profits tax on AI companies); addresses tax avoidance by AI corporations; links AI taxation to funding public services.

label: Ec Financial Inclusion
description: The extent to which the statement addresses financial inclusion and exclusion through AI, including algorithmic credit scoring, insurance pricing, and banking access. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific financial exclusion risks (algorithmic credit denial, discriminatory insurance pricing, automated loan rejection); mandates fairness audits in financial AI; requires alternative pathways for AI-excluded individuals.

label: Ec Global Value Chain Effects
description: The extent to which the statement addresses AI's impact on global supply chains, manufacturing, and the international division of labor. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI's reshoring effects, displacement of Global South manufacturing labor, or restructuring of global value chains; addresses content moderation labor in the Global South; proposes transition mechanisms for affected economies.

label: Ec Innovation Growth
description: The extent to which the statement emphasizes AI as a driver of innovation, productivity, and economic growth. Strong coverage includes: centers AI policy around enabling innovation and competitiveness; opposes regulation that might slow AI development; frames AI governance primarily in terms of economic opportunity; invokes national competitive advantage.

label: Ec Intellectual Property
description: The extent to which the statement addresses IP rights in AI contexts, including training data rights, AI-generated output ownership, and patent implications. Strong coverage includes: takes a specific position on AI training data copyright, AI-generated content ownership, or AI patent eligibility; proposes new IP frameworks for the AI era; addresses creators' rights vis-a-vis AI training.

label: Ec Job Transition Retraining
description: The extent to which the statement addresses worker retraining, transition support, and workforce development in response to AI-driven automation. Strong coverage includes: mandates employer-funded retraining programs; proposes sector-specific transition plans; establishes advance-notice requirements for AI-driven layoffs; funds public workforce development for AI-displaced workers.

label: Ec Market Efficiency Competition
description: The extent to which the statement addresses competitive dynamics in AI markets, including barriers to entry, interoperability, and market concentration. Strong coverage includes: demands antitrust enforcement in AI markets; mandates interoperability or data portability; identifies specific market concentration concerns (compute, data, foundation models); proposes structural remedies.

label: Ec North South Asymmetries
description: The extent to which the statement addresses technology gaps, dependencies, and power asymmetries between Global North and Global South in AI development and governance. Strong coverage includes: identifies structural North-South AI asymmetries (compute concentration, data extraction, brain drain, standard-setting dominance); proposes technology transfer, capacity building, or South-led AI governance; references digital colonialism.

label: Ec Open Source Public Ai
description: The extent to which the statement advocates for open-source AI development, public AI infrastructure, or commons-based approaches to AI. Strong coverage includes: demands public investment in open-source AI models; proposes national or public AI compute infrastructure; frames AI as a public good requiring commons-based governance; opposes proprietary lock-in of foundational AI capabilities.

label: Ec Platform Rent Extraction
description: The extent to which the statement addresses platform monopoly rent extraction, including app store fees, API pricing, and intermediary power in AI ecosystems. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific platform rent-extraction mechanisms (app store commissions, API paywalls, data lock-in); proposes interoperability mandates, platform utility regulation, or platform cooperativism; references platform capitalism critique.

label: Ec Procurement Fairness
description: The extent to which the statement addresses fairness, transparency, and accountability in government and institutional AI procurement. Strong coverage includes: mandates open, competitive AI procurement processes; requires algorithmic impact assessments before procurement; demands vendor transparency and accountability; prohibits sole-source AI contracts above thresholds.

label: Ec Small Business Access
description: The extent to which the statement addresses small and medium enterprise access to AI tools, markets, and infrastructure. Strong coverage includes: mandates SME access programs for AI; proposes public AI infrastructure or shared compute for small businesses; addresses AI vendor lock-in; requires AI-literate business support services.

label: Ec Surveillance Capitalism Economic
description: The extent to which the statement critiques the economic logic of surveillance capitalism — the extraction and commodification of behavioral data for prediction and profit. Strong coverage includes: explicitly references surveillance capitalism (Zuboff) or equivalent critique; identifies behavioral surplus extraction as a structural economic logic; proposes structural alternatives (ban behavioral advertising, data minimization mandates, public alternatives).

label: Ec Technology Conditionality
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the use of AI technology access as a condition in aid, trade, or diplomatic relationships. Strong coverage includes: identifies AI technology conditionality in trade agreements, development aid, or diplomatic relations; critiques or endorses technology transfer restrictions (e.g., chip export controls); addresses AI governance requirements as trade conditions.

label: Ec Technology Transfer Capacity
description: The extent to which the statement addresses building domestic AI production capacity versus consuming imported AI technology. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses technology transfer mechanisms; proposes building domestic AI chip fabrication, model training infrastructure, or national AI platforms; frames local production capacity as strategic priority.

label: Ep Affected Community Input
description: Affected community input: consulting those impacted by AI systems

label: Ep Algorithmic Auditing
description: Algorithmic auditing: systematic examination of AI system behavior

label: Ep Bias Testing Evaluation
description: Bias testing and evaluation: measuring and mitigating discrimination

label: Ep Capability Evaluation
description: Capability evaluation: assessing what AI systems can and cannot do

label: Ep Compute Governance
description: Compute governance: controlling access to AI training resources

label: Ep Dataset Documentation
description: Dataset documentation: datasheets, data cards, provenance records

label: Ep Differential Privacy
description: Differential privacy: mathematical privacy guarantees in AI

label: Ep Evidence Based Policy
description: Evidence-based policy: grounding AI rules in empirical evidence

label: Ep Explainability Xai
description: Explainable AI (XAI): methods for making AI decisions understandable

label: Ep Federated Learning
description: Federated learning: training AI without centralizing data

label: Ep Human In Loop Design
description: Human-in-the-loop design: systems requiring human involvement

label: Ep Interpretability Technical
description: Technical interpretability: research into understanding model internals

label: Ep Model Cards
description: Model cards: standardized documentation of AI model capabilities and limitations

label: Ep Open Data Standards
description: Open data standards: shared, accessible data for AI training and evaluation

label: Ep Open Weights Access
description: Open weights access: public access to AI model parameters

label: Ep Participatory Design
description: Participatory design: including affected users in AI system design

label: Ep Pre Registration
description: Pre-registration: committing to research design before data collection

label: Ep Privacy Preserving Techniques
description: Privacy-preserving AI techniques: protecting data while enabling AI

label: Ep Public Consultation
description: Public consultation: broad public input into AI policy

label: Ep Red Teaming Adversarial
description: Red teaming: adversarial testing for vulnerabilities and failure modes

label: Ep Reproducibility
description: Reproducibility: ability to replicate AI research and results

label: Ep Safety Benchmarking
description: Safety benchmarking: standardized tests for safety properties

label: Ep Scientific Peer Review
description: Scientific peer review: independent scientific scrutiny of AI claims

label: Ep Secure Multiparty
description: Secure multiparty computation: collaborative AI without data exposure

label: Ep Synthetic Data
description: Synthetic data: AI-generated data for training and testing

label: Ep System Cards
description: System cards: documentation of complete AI system context and use

label: Ep Uncertainty Quantification
description: Uncertainty quantification: AI knowing and communicating what it doesn't know

label: Ep Watermarking Provenance
description: Watermarking and provenance: tracking AI-generated content origins

label: Ev Ai Carbon Footprint
description: Energy consumption and carbon emissions from AI training, inference, and infrastructure. Strong coverage includes: quantifies or critically analyzes AI energy costs; discusses training compute emissions, data center impacts, or proposes carbon budgets for AI development.

label: Ev Ai For Environmental Monitoring
description: Use of AI as a tool for environmental monitoring, remote sensing, and ecological assessment. Strong coverage includes: details AI applications in environmental monitoring (satellite imagery analysis, sensor networks, pollution tracking); evaluates effectiveness or proposes deployment frameworks.

label: Ev Biodiversity Protection
description: AI's role in protecting or threatening biodiversity and ecosystem health. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI applications for biodiversity monitoring, species protection, or habitat conservation; or critiques AI infrastructure impacts on ecosystems.

label: Ev Climate Change Mitigation
description: AI's role in climate change mitigation, including climate modeling, emissions reduction, and clean energy optimization. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI as tool for climate mitigation in depth; discusses climate modeling, grid optimization, or emissions tracking; proposes AI-climate policy integration.

label: Ev Environmental Justice
description: Intersection of environmental harms and social equity in AI deployment, including disproportionate impacts on marginalized communities. Strong coverage includes: analyzes how AI environmental costs (data centers, e-waste, mining) disproportionately burden marginalized communities; proposes environmental justice frameworks for AI siting and supply chains.

label: Ev Green Ai Standards
description: Standards and benchmarks for energy-efficient AI development, training, and deployment. Strong coverage includes: proposes or evaluates specific green AI standards; discusses compute-efficiency metrics, energy reporting mandates, or model compression for sustainability.

label: Ev Nature Rights
description: Recognition of ecosystem or nature rights in AI governance, including legal personhood for natural entities. Strong coverage includes: invokes rights-of-nature frameworks for AI governance; proposes legal standing for ecosystems in AI impact decisions or nature-centered AI design principles.

label: Ev Planetary Boundaries
description: AI governance situated within planetary boundaries framework, respecting Earth system limits. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes planetary boundaries or Earth system limits as constraints on AI development; proposes governance aligned with biophysical thresholds.

label: Ev Resource Sustainability
description: Sustainable resource use in AI hardware supply chains, including rare earth minerals, water, and materials. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI hardware supply chain sustainability; discusses rare earth extraction, water consumption for cooling, or proposes sustainable sourcing standards.

label: Ga Audit Certification
description: Audit and certification: ongoing compliance auditing of AI systems

label: Ga Civil Liability Regime
description: The extent to which the statement addresses tort law adaptation, burden of proof, and compensation frameworks for AI-caused harms. Strong coverage includes: proposes specific civil liability rules for AI harms; addresses burden of proof allocation, strict vs. fault-based liability, or compensation mechanisms; references product liability adaptation for AI.

label: Ga Co Regulation
description: Extent to which the statement proposes a co-regulatory approach combining government authority with industry participation. Strong coverage includes: defines a co-regulatory structure where government sets goals and industry develops implementation standards; specifies roles, accountability, and oversight mechanisms.

label: Ga Constitutional Grounding
description: The extent to which the statement anchors AI governance in constitutional rights, principles, or frameworks. Strong coverage includes: explicitly grounds AI governance in constitutional provisions; invokes constitutional rights (dignity, equality, privacy) as binding constraints on AI; proposes constitutional amendments or interpretations for AI.

label: Ga Extraterritorial Application
description: Extraterritorial application: rules applying beyond national borders

label: Ga Foundation Model Governance
description: Foundation model governance: specific rules for large base models

label: Ga General Purpose Ai Rules
description: General-purpose AI rules: horizontal regulation for foundation models

label: Ga Horizon Scanning Foresight
description: The extent to which the statement proposes institutional capability for monitoring emerging AI developments and anticipating future risks. Strong coverage includes: proposes dedicated horizon scanning bodies, technology assessment offices, or foresight units for AI; mandates periodic technology outlook reports; establishes early warning systems for emerging AI risks.

label: Ga Incident Reporting
description: Incident reporting: mandatory reporting of AI failures or harms

label: Ga International Harmonization
description: International harmonization: aligning AI rules across jurisdictions

label: Ga Interoperability Standards
description: Interoperability standards: technical standards enabling AI compatibility

label: Ga Liability Allocation
description: Liability allocation: who bears legal responsibility for AI harms

label: Ga Lifecycle Governance
description: The extent to which the statement addresses governance requirements across the full AI lifecycle from design through deployment to decommission. Strong coverage includes: proposes governance requirements spanning the full AI lifecycle; addresses design-phase obligations, deployment monitoring, and decommission/retirement procedures; mandates lifecycle documentation.

label: Ga Mandatory Ai Insurance
description: The extent to which the statement proposes compulsory insurance schemes for AI operators or deployers. Strong coverage includes: explicitly proposes mandatory insurance for AI systems; specifies coverage requirements, risk pools, or insurance obligations for high-risk AI applications.

label: Ga Mandatory Impact Assessment
description: Mandatory impact assessments: DPIA, algorithmic impact assessments required

label: Ga Market Surveillance
description: Market surveillance: post-market monitoring by regulators

label: Ga Mutual Recognition
description: Mutual recognition: accepting other countries' regulatory approvals

label: Ga Open Source Treatment
description: Open source AI treatment: how open-weight models are regulated

label: Ga Post Deployment Monitoring
description: Extent to which the statement mandates ongoing monitoring of AI systems after deployment. Strong coverage includes: specifies post-market surveillance obligations; defines monitoring metrics, reporting frequency, and triggers for system withdrawal or modification.

label: Ga Pre Deployment Approval
description: Pre-deployment approval: regulatory sign-off before release

label: Ga Private Right Of Action
description: The extent to which the statement establishes that individuals can bring legal action against AI harms. Strong coverage includes: explicitly establishes private right of action for AI harms; specifies standing requirements, available remedies, or procedural rules for individual lawsuits against AI operators.

label: Ga Public Register Transparency
description: Public registers: databases of AI systems, transparency registers

label: Ga Redress Mechanisms
description: Redress mechanisms: pathways for victims of AI harm to seek compensation

label: Ga Regulatory Capture Concern
description: The extent to which the statement expresses concern that AI regulators may be captured by the industry they regulate. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses regulatory capture risks; proposes structural safeguards (revolving door restrictions, conflict of interest rules, independent funding); analyzes industry influence on AI regulation.

label: Ga Risk Classification Tiering
description: Risk classification: tiered approach to AI risk (high/medium/low risk categories)

label: Ga Safe Harbor Provisions
description: Safe harbor provisions: liability protection under certain conditions

label: Ga Sandbox Regulatory Flexibility
description: Regulatory sandboxes: safe experimentation spaces with relaxed rules

label: Ga Sector Specific Rules
description: Sector-specific AI rules: tailored regulation by industry sector

label: Ga Self Declaration Compliance
description: Self-declaration: companies attest their own compliance

label: Ga Shareholder Investor Enforcement
description: The extent to which the statement addresses capital-market mechanisms for AI accountability, including shareholder activism and investor oversight. Strong coverage includes: proposes shareholder or investor-driven AI governance mechanisms; addresses AI risk disclosure requirements for investors, ESG integration of AI risks, or fiduciary duty extensions for AI oversight.

label: Ga Supply Chain Accountability
description: Supply chain accountability: upstream/downstream responsibility

label: Ga Third Party Oversight
description: Third-party oversight: external auditors, independent assessors

label: Ga Voluntary Commitment
description: Voluntary commitments: industry pledges, non-binding agreements

label: Ga Whistleblower Protection
description: Extent to which the statement provides protections for individuals reporting AI-related misconduct or harms. Strong coverage includes: establishes specific whistleblower protections for AI contexts; defines protected disclosures, anti-retaliation measures, and reporting channels.

label: Gi Ai Sovereignty
description: The extent to which the statement asserts national sovereignty over AI models, training data, and computational infrastructure. Strong coverage includes: explicitly asserts AI sovereignty; proposes national control over AI training data, model development, or compute infrastructure; frames data localization or model sovereignty as national security priorities.

label: Gi Algorithmic Self Determination
description: Right of colonized or marginalized communities to control algorithms that govern their lives. Strong coverage includes: argues for community control over algorithmic systems affecting them; proposes algorithmic impact assessments led by affected communities or indigenous data governance protocols.

label: Gi Anti Eurocentrism
description: Critique of Eurocentric or Western-centric norms in AI ethics and governance frameworks. Strong coverage includes: explicitly critiques Eurocentrism in AI ethics; proposes alternative normative foundations from non-Western traditions; deconstructs universalist claims as culturally specific.

label: Gi Arms Control Ai Nonproliferation
description: Arms control and nonproliferation frameworks applied to AI capabilities. Strong coverage includes: proposes AI arms control regimes, nonproliferation treaties, or export controls on AI capabilities; analyzes AI arms race dynamics with specific control mechanisms.

label: Gi Authoritarian Governance Rationale
description: Authoritarian framing of AI governance emphasizing stability, performance legitimacy, social order, or state-directed innovation. Strong coverage includes: articulates or analyzes authoritarian AI governance rationale (social stability, performance legitimacy, state-led development); engages with non-liberal governance models substantively.

label: Gi Data Colonialism
description: Critique of data extraction from the Global South and marginalized communities by powerful actors. Strong coverage includes: explicitly uses data colonialism framework; analyzes extractive data practices from Global South or indigenous communities; proposes data sovereignty or anti-extractive governance.

label: Gi Development Equity
description: Equitable AI development for developing nations, including technology transfer, capacity building, and bridging the AI divide. Strong coverage includes: centrally analyzes the AI divide between Global North and South; proposes technology transfer mechanisms, capacity building, or equitable development frameworks.

label: Gi Digital Sovereignty
description: National or regional control over digital infrastructure, data flows, and platform governance. Strong coverage includes: analyzes digital sovereignty strategies (data localization, sovereign cloud, platform regulation); proposes frameworks for national/regional control over digital infrastructure.

label: Gi Epistemic Sovereignty
description: Right of peoples and nations to produce knowledge on their own terms, resisting epistemic dependence on dominant AI paradigms. Strong coverage includes: invokes epistemic sovereignty or postcolonial knowledge politics; critiques dependence on Western AI knowledge production; proposes indigenous or local epistemic frameworks for AI.

label: Gi Global Commons
description: Framing of AI, data, or foundational models as global commons requiring shared governance. Strong coverage includes: explicitly argues AI or data should be governed as global commons; proposes shared governance structures, commons licensing, or anti-enclosure mechanisms.

label: Gi Human Security
description: Human security paradigm (broader than state security) applied to AI governance, encompassing food, health, economic, environmental, personal, community, and political security. Strong coverage includes: invokes human security framework explicitly; analyzes AI impacts across multiple security dimensions (food, health, economic, personal); proposes people-centered AI security governance.

label: Gi International Cooperation
description: International cooperation, multilateral governance, and harmonization of AI standards across nations. Strong coverage includes: advocates multilateral AI governance frameworks; analyzes international treaty mechanisms, harmonization efforts, or proposes new international AI institutions.

label: Gi Military Ai Governance
description: Governance of military AI, including lethal autonomous weapons systems (LAWS), autonomous targeting, and military AI ethics. Strong coverage includes: focuses on LAWS governance, autonomous weapons bans, or military AI ethics; analyzes meaningful human control, CCW processes, or military AI accountability.

label: Gi Multi Polarity
description: Multi-polar world order in AI governance, resisting unipolar dominance by any single power. Strong coverage includes: advocates multi-polar AI governance architecture; analyzes US-China duopoly risks; proposes governance structures ensuring diverse power centers in AI standard-setting.

label: Gi National Sovereignty
description: National sovereignty over AI governance, including the right of states to regulate AI within their jurisdiction. Strong coverage includes: centrally argues for national regulatory autonomy over AI; analyzes sovereignty tensions with transnational AI firms; proposes sovereign AI strategies or national AI acts.

label: Gi Non Interference
description: Non-interference principle in AI governance, respecting states' internal regulatory autonomy. Strong coverage includes: invokes non-interference principle to resist external AI governance imposition; analyzes tensions between international standards and domestic regulatory autonomy.

label: Gi Peace Security Civilian
description: AI governance oriented toward peace, civilian protection, and conflict prevention. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI's role in peace processes, civilian protection, or conflict escalation; proposes AI governance to prevent civilian harm in conflict zones.

label: Gi Regulatory Sovereignty
description: The extent to which the statement asserts the right to set autonomous AI regulatory frameworks versus accepting imported standards. Strong coverage includes: explicitly asserts regulatory sovereignty; resists harmonization pressures from dominant AI powers; proposes nationally or regionally tailored regulatory frameworks independent of EU/US models.

label: Gp Bilateral Cooperation
description: Bilateral cooperation: specific country-to-country AI agreements

label: Gp Capacity Building Emphasis
description: Capacity building emphasis: building AI governance institutions globally

label: Gp Chinese Model Alignment
description: Chinese model alignment: reflects Chinese governance approach to AI

label: Gp Competitive Ai Race
description: Competitive AI race framing: AI as geopolitical competition

label: Gp Cooperative Ai Development
description: Cooperative AI development: collaborative international approach

label: Gp Eu Brussels Effect
description: EU Brussels Effect: explicitly adopts or advocates EU regulatory approach

label: Gp Global South Advocacy
description: Global South advocacy: centered on developing country interests

label: Gp Multilateral Un System
description: Multilateral UN system: engagement with UN AI governance

label: Gp Non Aligned Positioning
description: Non-aligned positioning: explicitly avoiding bloc alignment

label: Gp Open Internet Advocacy
description: Open internet advocacy: free flow of AI across borders

label: Gp Regional Bloc Approach
description: Regional bloc approach: ASEAN, AU, etc. regional coordination

label: Gp Sanctions Export Controls
description: Sanctions and export controls: restricting AI technology flows

label: Gp Splinternet Acceptance
description: Splinternet acceptance: acceptance of fragmented internet/AI governance

label: Gp Technology Transfer Advocacy
description: Technology transfer advocacy: sharing AI with developing nations

label: Gp Us Market Led
description: US market-led approach: light-touch regulation, industry self-governance

label: Gp Western Liberal Alignment
description: Western liberal alignment: document reflects Western liberal democratic norms

label: Hr Childrens Rights
description: The extent to which the statement addresses children's best interests and protection from AI harms, including data collection, profiling, and age-inappropriate content. Strong coverage includes: invokes CRC (Convention on the Rights of the Child); mandates age-appropriate design, prohibits behavioral profiling of children, addresses AI in education and child welfare; requires child impact assessments.

label: Hr Disability Rights
description: The extent to which the statement addresses disability rights and reasonable accommodation in AI design, deployment, and governance. Strong coverage includes: invokes CRPD; mandates accessibility-by-design in AI systems; addresses AI discrimination against disabled persons (hiring algorithms, insurance, benefits); requires assistive AI development.

label: Hr Equal Protection
description: The extent to which the statement demands equal protection under law in algorithmic decision-making, particularly in criminal justice, welfare, immigration, and public services. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific domains (criminal sentencing, welfare eligibility, immigration screening) where algorithmic decisions must meet equal-protection standards; mandates disparate-impact analysis; requires judicial or administrative review.

label: Hr Freedom Expression
description: The extent to which the statement addresses freedom of speech and expression in the context of algorithmic content moderation, recommendation systems, and AI-generated content. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses algorithmic censorship, content moderation bias, or chilling effects of AI surveillance on speech; mandates transparency in content moderation algorithms; opposes AI-enabled suppression of dissent.

label: Hr Freedom Religion
description: The extent to which the statement addresses religious freedom in AI-mediated contexts, including algorithmic profiling based on religion and AI's impact on religious practice. Strong coverage includes: explicitly prohibits AI-based religious profiling or surveillance of religious communities; addresses AI content moderation of religious expression; considers religious accommodation in AI systems.

label: Hr Freedom Thought
description: The extent to which the statement protects cognitive liberty, mental privacy, and freedom from AI-driven manipulation of beliefs, preferences, or emotions. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes cognitive liberty or mental privacy; opposes neuro-surveillance, emotion recognition for manipulation, or AI systems designed to alter beliefs without awareness; references brain-computer interface governance.

label: Hr Human Alternative Right
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to request a human decision-maker instead of an AI system. Strong coverage includes: explicitly mandates the right to a human alternative; requires human decision-makers to be available upon request; specifies contexts where human alternatives are mandatory (healthcare, justice, employment).

label: Hr Indigenous Peoples Rights
description: The extent to which the statement addresses indigenous peoples' rights to self-determination, cultural preservation, and data sovereignty in AI contexts. Strong coverage includes: invokes UNDRIP; addresses indigenous data sovereignty (OCAP, CARE principles); mandates free, prior, and informed consent for AI affecting indigenous communities; opposes extractive data practices on indigenous knowledge.

label: Hr Minority Rights
description: The extent to which the statement addresses protection of racial, ethnic, linguistic, and other minority groups from AI harms and ensures their representation in AI governance. Strong coverage includes: identifies specific minority groups facing AI harms (racial profiling, linguistic exclusion, cultural erasure); mandates disaggregated impact data; requires minority representation in AI governance bodies.

label: Hr Nondiscrimination
description: The extent to which the statement invokes non-discrimination and equality before the law as human rights principles constraining AI systems. Strong coverage includes: invokes ICERD, CEDAW, CRPD, or other anti-discrimination treaties; identifies specific AI discrimination vectors (race, gender, disability, age); mandates algorithmic impact assessments on protected groups.

label: Hr Right To Contest Automated
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to challenge and override automated decisions, including the right to opt out of algorithmic processing. Strong coverage includes: mandates the right to human review of automated decisions; establishes opt-out rights from algorithmic processing; specifies appeal procedures and timelines; requires that contested decisions be suspended pending review.

label: Hr Right To Cultural Participation
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the right to participate in cultural life in the digital/AI age, including protection of cultural heritage, linguistic diversity, and creative expression. Strong coverage includes: addresses AI's impact on cultural diversity, linguistic preservation, or creative expression; opposes cultural homogenization through AI; mandates multilingual AI development; protects cultural heritage from AI extraction.

label: Hr Right To Education
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the right to education, including digital literacy, AI literacy, and equitable access to AI-enhanced learning. Strong coverage includes: mandates AI literacy curricula in public education; demands equitable access to AI learning tools; addresses risks of AI-driven educational sorting or tracking; invokes right to education (ICESCR Art. 13).

label: Hr Right To Explanation
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to receive a meaningful explanation after an automated decision has been made. Strong coverage includes: mandates individualized, understandable explanations of automated decisions; specifies what constitutes a meaningful explanation (factors, weights, alternatives); requires explanations in plain language; sets response timelines.

label: Hr Right To Health
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the right to health in AI contexts, including AI in medical diagnosis, drug discovery, health data, and equitable healthcare access. Strong coverage includes: invokes right to health (ICESCR Art. 12); addresses AI diagnostic bias in healthcare, health data governance, or equitable access to AI-enhanced medicine; mandates clinical validation and regulatory approval for AI health tools.

label: Hr Right To Housing
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the right to housing in AI contexts, including algorithmic tenant screening, dynamic pricing, and AI-driven gentrification. Strong coverage includes: identifies algorithmic tenant screening, rent-setting algorithms, or AI-driven housing discrimination as rights concerns; mandates fair housing audits for AI systems; addresses automated eviction decisions.

label: Hr Right To Life
description: The extent to which the statement addresses protection of life and physical security in AI contexts, including lethal autonomous weapons and AI in critical infrastructure. Strong coverage includes: explicitly opposes lethal autonomous weapons systems (LAWS); invokes IHRL Article 6 (right to life); demands human authorization for any AI system with lethal capability; addresses AI in critical safety systems.

label: Hr Right To Political Participation
description: The extent to which the statement protects democratic participation from AI manipulation, including micro-targeting, deepfakes in elections, and algorithmic suppression of political speech. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses AI-driven political micro-targeting, deepfake threats to elections, or algorithmic voter suppression; mandates transparency in political AI use; calls for regulation of AI in campaigns.

label: Hr Right To Privacy
description: The extent to which the statement frames privacy as a fundamental human right rather than merely a data-protection compliance matter, invoking bodily autonomy, spatial privacy, and informational self-determination. Strong coverage includes: invokes privacy as an inalienable human right (UDHR Art. 12, ICCPR Art. 17); addresses mass surveillance, biometric identification in public spaces, or predictive policing as human rights violations; demands moratoriums on invasive AI surveillance.

label: Hr Right To Refuse Ai
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to opt out of AI-mediated processes entirely. Strong coverage includes: explicitly establishes the right to refuse AI interaction; mandates non-AI alternatives in essential services; prohibits compulsory AI-mediated processes without consent.

label: Hr Right To Remedy
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to effective legal remedy for harms caused by AI systems, including access to courts, compensation, and systemic relief. Strong coverage includes: mandates accessible judicial or quasi-judicial remedies for AI harms; specifies burden-of-proof allocation (shifting to deployer); addresses collective redress and class actions; establishes compensation frameworks.

label: Hr Right To Social Protection
description: The extent to which the statement addresses social safety nets and welfare protections in the context of AI-driven economic disruption. Strong coverage includes: demands strengthened social protection systems to address AI displacement; proposes specific mechanisms (UBI, portable benefits, social insurance reform); invokes ICESCR Art. 9 (right to social security).

label: Hr Right To Transparency Notice
description: The extent to which the statement establishes the right to be informed when AI is being used in decisions affecting an individual. Strong coverage includes: mandates disclosure whenever AI is used in consequential decisions; requires labeling of AI-generated content; specifies timing and format of notice; addresses covert AI use as a rights violation.

label: Hr Right To Work
description: The extent to which the statement addresses labor rights and the right to work in the context of AI-driven automation and workplace transformation. Strong coverage includes: invokes right to work (ICESCR Art. 6); demands impact assessments before AI-driven workforce displacement; mandates transition support, retraining, or new employment guarantees; addresses gig-economy labor rights.

label: Pd Appeal Procedures
description: Appeal procedures: formal process for challenging decisions

label: Pd Aspirational Only
description: Aspirational only: vague principles without concrete commitments

label: Pd Cross Sector Coordination
description: Cross-sector coordination: linking AI to other policy domains

label: Pd Definitions Provided
description: Definitions provided: clear definitions of key terms including 'AI'

label: Pd Enforcement Mechanisms
description: Enforcement mechanisms: penalties, sanctions, consequences

label: Pd Evidence Base Cited
description: Evidence base cited: references to empirical research

label: Pd Exceptions Carve Outs
description: Exceptions and carve-outs: explicit exclusions from requirements

label: Pd Hard Law Tools
description: Hard law tools: regulations, statutes, binding rules

label: Pd High Specificity
description: High specificity: concrete, detailed, actionable provisions

label: Pd Implementation Roadmap
description: Implementation roadmap: clear steps and milestones

label: Pd Legislative Hooks
description: Legislative hooks: references to or calls for legislation

label: Pd Monitoring Framework
description: Monitoring framework: tracking progress and outcomes

label: Pd Pilot Programs
description: Pilot programs: experimental or phased implementation

label: Pd Resource Commitments
description: Resource commitments: funding, staffing, infrastructure

label: Pd Review Cycle
description: Review cycle: scheduled reassessment of the document

label: Pd Scope Limitations Stated
description: Scope limitations stated: explicit statement of what is covered

label: Pd Soft Law Tools
description: Soft law tools: guidelines, codes, voluntary commitments

label: Pd Stakeholder Consultation
description: Stakeholder consultation: evidence of inclusive process

label: Pd Sunset Clauses
description: Sunset clauses: automatic expiration provisions

label: Pd Timeline Commitments
description: Timeline commitments: specific dates and deadlines

label: Pd Worker Codetermination
description: The extent to which the statement mandates legally binding worker participation in AI deployment decisions. Strong coverage includes: explicitly mandates worker co-determination in AI decisions; proposes works council consultation rights, collective bargaining over AI, or legally binding worker veto on workplace AI deployments.

label: Ph Abrahamic Stewardship Khilafa
description: Abrahamic stewardship and trusteeship: humans as stewards (khilafa) or created in God's image (imago Dei), bearing responsibility for creation including technology. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes khilafa or imago Dei; argues human stewardship responsibility extends to AI; proposes theological grounding for AI governance obligations.

label: Ph Ahimsa Nonharm
description: Ahimsa (non-harm) from Dharmic traditions (Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism) applied to AI ethics. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes ahimsa; applies non-harm across all sentient beings to AI; proposes AI design principles grounded in Dharmic non-violence.

label: Ph Care Ethics Relationships
description: Ethics of care applied to AI: relational ethics, responsiveness to vulnerability, and caring relationships. Strong coverage includes: applies care ethics framework; analyzes AI through lens of caring relationships, attentiveness, responsibility, and responsiveness; critiques abstract justice approaches.

label: Ph Categorical Imperative Kant
description: Kantian deontological principles: treating persons as ends, universalizability, moral autonomy. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Kant; applies categorical imperative, humanity formula, or universalizability test to AI; argues AI must never reduce persons to mere means.

label: Ph Catholic Social Teaching
description: Catholic Social Teaching applied to AI: subsidiarity, solidarity, preferential option for the poor, and common good. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes CST principles (subsidiarity, solidarity, common good); applies papal encyclicals or Vatican AI statements to AI governance; proposes CST-aligned policy.

label: Ph Contextual Integrity Nissenbaum
description: Context-appropriate information flows: AI systems must respect established informational norms of specific social contexts. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Nissenbaum's contextual integrity; analyzes AI data flows against context-relative norms; proposes CI-based privacy frameworks for AI.

label: Ph Covenant Restorative Justice
description: Covenantal justice and restorative justice rooted in Hebrew tzedek: justice as right-relationship, repair, and covenant faithfulness. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes tzedek, covenantal justice, or restorative justice in Abrahamic tradition; proposes repair-centered AI accountability grounded in relational justice theology.

label: Ph Dharmic Order Accountability
description: Dharmic order (dharma/karma) applied to AI: cosmic accountability, moral causation, and duty-based action. Strong coverage includes: invokes dharma or karma frameworks; applies moral causation and duty concepts to AI accountability; proposes dharma-aligned AI governance.

label: Ph Distributive Justice
description: Fair distribution of AI's benefits and burdens across society. Strong coverage includes: centrally analyzes distributional impacts of AI; proposes specific redistribution mechanisms (taxation, benefit sharing, access mandates) or equity metrics.

label: Ph Do No Harm
description: Non-maleficence principle: AI systems should not cause harm; harm prevention takes priority. Strong coverage includes: non-maleficence is a central organizing principle; analyzes harm types, thresholds, and trade-offs; proposes harm prevention mechanisms or red lines.

label: Ph Epistemic Injustice Fricker
description: Testimonial and hermeneutical injustice in AI: systems that systematically discredit certain knowers or lack interpretive resources for marginalized experiences. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Fricker or epistemic injustice; analyzes AI systems as sites of testimonial/hermeneutical injustice; proposes epistemic justice interventions.

label: Ph Fiduciary Stewardship Duty
description: Fiduciary or stewardship obligations for AI developers and deployers toward affected persons. Strong coverage includes: proposes information fiduciary or stewardship duties for AI actors; analyzes trust relationships between AI developers and users; argues for legal fiduciary frameworks.

label: Ph Human Capabilities Sen Nussbaum
description: Capabilities approach to AI justice: AI should expand human capabilities and freedoms, not merely increase utility. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Sen or Nussbaum; applies capabilities list to AI assessment; argues AI policy should be evaluated by capability expansion for the least capable.

label: Ph Interfaith Convergence
description: The extent to which the statement promotes multi-faith collaborative approaches to AI governance. Strong coverage includes: explicitly proposes interfaith collaboration on AI ethics; identifies shared values across religious traditions as basis for AI governance; organizes or references multi-faith AI dialogues or joint statements.

label: Ph Intergenerational Ethics
description: Ethical obligations to future generations in AI development and governance. Strong coverage includes: applies formal intergenerational ethics (Jonas, Parfit, or similar); argues for long-term thinking in AI governance; proposes future-generation representation mechanisms.

label: Ph Islamic Maslaha Adl Amanah
description: Islamic public interest (maslaha), justice (adl), and trusteeship (amanah) applied to AI governance. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes maslaha, adl, or amanah; applies Islamic jurisprudential principles to AI; proposes Shariah-compatible AI governance frameworks.

label: Ph Islamic Prohibition Gharar
description: Islamic prohibition of excessive uncertainty (gharar) applied to AI: requiring transparency and prohibiting deceptive algorithmic opacity. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes gharar prohibition; argues AI opacity violates Islamic principles of transparency in transactions; proposes gharar-based explainability requirements.

label: Ph Islamic Shura Consultative
description: Islamic consultative governance (shura) applied to AI: collective deliberation and consensus-seeking. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes shura; proposes consultative AI governance models grounded in Islamic deliberation tradition; analyzes parallels with participatory governance.

label: Ph Justice As Fairness Rawls
description: Rawlsian justice applied to AI: veil of ignorance, difference principle, fair equality of opportunity. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Rawls; applies veil of ignorance or difference principle to AI design; argues AI benefits must flow to least advantaged.

label: Ph Moral Pluralism
description: Acceptance of multiple valid moral frameworks; AI governance must accommodate irreducible value diversity. Strong coverage includes: explicitly argues for moral pluralism in AI; proposes governance mechanisms that accommodate multiple ethical frameworks; critiques single-framework approaches.

label: Ph Moral Universalism
description: Commitment to universal moral principles applicable across all cultures in AI governance. Strong coverage includes: explicitly argues for universal moral principles in AI; defends cross-cultural validity of core norms (dignity, rights); critiques moral relativism in AI ethics.

label: Ph Precautionary Principle
description: Precautionary approach to AI risks: where potential harms are serious, lack of certainty should not delay protective action. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes precautionary principle; argues for burden-of-proof reversal on AI developers; proposes moratoriums, mandatory risk assessment, or safety-first deployment rules.

label: Ph Sacred Knowledge Protection
description: Protection of sacred, ceremonial, and indigenous knowledge systems from AI extraction, digitization, or misappropriation. Strong coverage includes: argues for protections of sacred or indigenous knowledge from AI training data extraction; proposes indigenous data sovereignty protocols or sacred knowledge firewalls.

label: Ph Sanctity Of Human Life
description: Sanctity of human life as non-tradeable threshold: AI must never be permitted to devalue or commodify human life. Strong coverage includes: invokes sanctity of life as absolute constraint on AI; argues certain AI applications (lethal autonomous weapons, life-and-death triage) violate inviolable human dignity.

label: Ph Social Contract Consent
description: Social contract theory applied to AI: legitimate governance requires consent of the governed. Strong coverage includes: explicitly applies social contract theory to AI governance; argues for consent-based legitimacy, proposes new social contracts for the AI age, or analyzes democratic mandate for AI regulation.

label: Ph Solidarity
description: Solidarity as ethical principle in AI governance, emphasizing mutual aid, collective responsibility, and social bonds (European social tradition). Strong coverage includes: invokes solidarity as a foundational principle; proposes collective responsibility frameworks, mutual aid structures, or solidarity-based AI governance mechanisms.

label: Ph Ubuntu Communal Accountability
description: Ubuntu-based communal accountability and restorative justice applied to AI governance. Strong coverage includes: applies Ubuntu accountability frameworks to AI; proposes restorative rather than punitive approaches to AI harms; argues for communal rather than individual liability models.

label: Ph Ubuntu Relational Personhood
description: Ubuntu philosophy: personhood is constituted through relationships; 'I am because we are' applied to AI design. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes Ubuntu; argues AI must support relational personhood; proposes community-centered AI design grounded in African relational ontology.

label: Ph Utilitarian Greatest Good
description: Utilitarian reasoning: maximizing aggregate welfare or wellbeing through AI. Strong coverage includes: explicitly applies utilitarian calculus to AI governance; analyzes welfare maximization, cost-benefit frameworks, or proposes aggregate wellbeing metrics for AI evaluation.

label: Ph Virtue Excellence
description: Virtue ethics applied to AI: cultivating human excellence, practical wisdom, and moral character in AI contexts. Strong coverage includes: applies virtue ethics framework to AI; discusses phronesis, character formation, or how AI supports/undermines human flourishing and moral development.

label: Ph Vulnerability Protection
description: Special ethical obligations to protect vulnerable persons (children, elderly, disabled, refugees) from AI harms. Strong coverage includes: centers vulnerability as an organizing ethical concept; identifies specific vulnerable populations and AI risks they face; proposes heightened protections or vulnerability-sensitive design.

label: Rh Autonomous Weapons Risk
description: Autonomous weapons risks: lethal autonomous systems

label: Rh Bioweapons Risk
description: Bioweapons risks: AI accelerating biological weapons development

label: Rh Catastrophic Risk
description: Catastrophic risks: AI causing large-scale civilizational harm

label: Rh Children Exploitation Risk
description: Children exploitation risks: AI used to exploit minors

label: Rh Concentration Power Risk
description: Concentration of power risks: AI enabling authoritarianism or monopoly

label: Rh Critical Infrastructure Risk
description: Critical infrastructure risks: AI attacks on power, water, finance

label: Rh Cultural Erasure Risk
description: Cultural erasure risks: AI homogenizing or erasing cultures

label: Rh Cyberattack Risk
description: Cyberattack risks: AI-enhanced offensive cyber capabilities

label: Rh Democratic Erosion Risk
description: Democratic erosion risks: AI undermining democratic institutions

label: Rh Discrimination Bias Harm
description: Discrimination and bias harms: unfair treatment by AI systems

label: Rh Dual Use Weapons Risk
description: Dual-use and weapons risks: AI repurposed for weapons

label: Rh Environmental Harm
description: Environmental harms: AI's ecological damage

label: Rh Epistemic Harm
description: Epistemic harms: AI damaging the quality of knowledge and discourse

label: Rh Existential Risk
description: Existential risks: AI posing threat to human survival

label: Rh Financial Harm
description: Financial harms: AI causing economic losses to individuals

label: Rh Gender Based Harm
description: Gender-based harms: AI disproportionately harming women/gender minorities

label: Rh Labor Displacement Harm
description: Labor displacement harms: job losses from AI automation

label: Rh Manipulation Persuasion Harm
description: Manipulation and persuasion harms: AI exploiting psychology

label: Rh Minority Community Harm
description: Minority community harms: AI causing disproportionate harm to minorities

label: Rh Misinformation Deepfake Harm
description: Misinformation and deepfake harms: AI-generated false content

label: Rh Physical Safety Harm
description: Physical safety harms: AI causing bodily harm

label: Rh Privacy Surveillance Harm
description: Privacy and surveillance harms: AI-enabled violations of privacy

label: Rh Psychological Harm
description: Psychological harms: AI causing mental health damage

label: Rh Surveillance State Risk
description: Surveillance state risks: AI-enabled authoritarian control

label: Rh Systemic Risk
description: Systemic risks: AI causing cascading failures in critical systems

label: Sc Ai Creative Arts Authorship
description: The extent to which the statement addresses AI disruption of artistic creation, authorship, and copyright in creative industries. Strong coverage includes: centrally addresses AI-generated art, music, or text; analyzes copyright implications, authorship attribution, or economic impact on creative workers; proposes legal or governance frameworks for AI creativity.

label: Sc Anti Disinformation
description: Combating AI-generated or AI-amplified misinformation, deepfakes, and epistemic manipulation. Strong coverage includes: focuses on AI-driven disinformation risks; proposes detection mechanisms, provenance standards, or platform governance interventions against synthetic media.

label: Sc Bodily Autonomy Reproductive Justice
description: Protection of bodily autonomy and reproductive rights in AI-mediated contexts (surveillance, health AI, predictive systems). Strong coverage includes: directly analyzes AI threats to bodily autonomy or reproductive rights; addresses fertility tracking surveillance, algorithmic pregnancy discrimination, or reproductive health AI bias.

label: Sc Community Self Determination
description: Right of communities to self-govern over local AI deployment and data use. Strong coverage includes: argues for community veto or consent mechanisms over local AI deployment; proposes community data trusts, local governance boards, or participatory technology assessment.

label: Sc Cultural Diversity Pluralism
description: Commitment to cultural and value pluralism in AI design, deployment, and governance. Strong coverage includes: central argument for value pluralism in AI; critiques monocultural AI norms; proposes participatory design across cultural contexts or pluralistic value alignment methods.

label: Sc Cultural Preservation
description: Protection of cultural heritage, traditions, and local knowledge systems from AI-driven homogenization. Strong coverage includes: explicitly argues AI risks cultural erasure or homogenization; proposes mechanisms for heritage protection, culturally-grounded datasets, or local knowledge safeguards.

label: Sc Cultural Values Protection
description: The extent to which the statement addresses protecting cultural, linguistic, or religious identity from AI-driven homogenization. Strong coverage includes: explicitly argues AI risks cultural or religious identity erosion; proposes mechanisms for protecting cultural values, local languages, or traditional practices from AI-driven homogenization; addresses algorithmic monoculture.

label: Sc Family Care Relationships
description: Protection of family bonds, caregiving relationships, and intimate social structures from AI disruption. Strong coverage includes: analyzes AI impacts on family dynamics, caregiving, or intimate relationships in depth; proposes safeguards for relational autonomy in domestic/care contexts.

label: Sc Intergenerational Equity
description: Fairness across generations in AI policy, including obligations to future generations and protection of children/youth. Strong coverage includes: explicitly frames AI governance as intergenerational issue; discusses long-term impacts on future generations or age-differentiated risks; proposes intergenerational impact assessments.

label: Sc Knowledge Equity Open Science
description: Equitable access to AI knowledge, open science principles, and democratization of AI research. Strong coverage includes: advocates open-source AI, open datasets, or open science mandates; critiques proprietary knowledge hoarding; proposes knowledge commons for AI.

label: Sc Language Equity
description: Linguistic diversity in AI systems and equitable access to language technologies across languages. Strong coverage includes: centrally addresses NLP resource gaps, language endangerment through AI, or proposes multilingual AI equity frameworks; discusses low-resource language inclusion strategies.

label: Sc Mental Health Wellbeing
description: Concern for mental health impacts of AI systems, including addictive design, surveillance anxiety, and algorithmic harm to psychological wellbeing. Strong coverage includes: directly investigates AI mental health impacts (addictive UX, content harms, surveillance stress); proposes wellbeing metrics, design standards, or therapeutic safeguards.

label: Sc Religious Freedom
description: Protection of religious freedom, conscience, and faith expression in AI-mediated contexts. Strong coverage includes: directly addresses AI impacts on religious practice, conscience rights, or faith-based accommodation; proposes protections for religious expression in algorithmic systems.

label: Sc Social Cohesion
description: Concern for maintaining social bonds and community solidarity in AI-mediated contexts. Strong coverage includes: central theme; argues AI threatens or must strengthen social bonds; proposes social impact assessments or community-level interventions.

label: Sc Social Mobility
description: AI effects on social mobility, class stratification, and economic opportunity structures. Strong coverage includes: centrally analyzes how AI reshapes class structures, opportunity ladders, or meritocratic pathways; proposes policies to prevent AI-driven stratification.

label: Sc Technology Gbv
description: AI's role in enabling or combating gender-based violence, including deepfake abuse, stalkerware, and online harassment. Strong coverage includes: centrally addresses technology-facilitated GBV; analyzes deepfake intimate imagery, AI stalkerware, algorithmic harassment amplification; proposes detection or legal remedies.

label: Sf Criminal Justice
description: Criminal justice sector: AI in policing, courts, corrections

label: Sf Critical Infrastructure
