description: Critical infrastructure: AI in power grids, water, communications

label: Sf Defense Security
description: Defense and security sector: military AI, national security applications

label: Sf Education
description: Education sector: AI in teaching, assessment, learning

label: Sf Employment Hr
description: Employment and HR sector: AI in hiring, performance management

label: Sf Financial Services
description: Financial services sector: AI in banking, insurance, credit

label: Sf Guardrail Circumvention
description: The extent to which the statement addresses the risk of AI safety measures being bypassed by end users. Strong coverage includes: explicitly addresses guardrail circumvention risks (jailbreaking, prompt injection, safety bypass techniques); proposes robust safety mechanisms resistant to user circumvention; mandates red-teaming for circumvention resilience.

label: Sf Healthcare
description: Healthcare sector: AI in medical diagnosis, treatment, healthcare delivery

label: Sf Media Entertainment
description: Media and entertainment sector: AI in content creation, distribution

label: Sf Public Administration
description: Public administration sector: AI in government services

label: Sf Scientific Research
description: Scientific research: AI in academic and R&D contexts

label: Sf Social Services
description: Social services: AI in welfare, benefits, social care

label: Sf Transport Autonomous
description: Transport and autonomous vehicles: self-driving, logistics AI

label: Sr Ai Education Training
description: AI education and training: formal curriculum, workforce skills

label: Sr Ai Hub Aspiration
description: The extent to which the statement expresses aspiration to become a regional or global AI center of excellence. Strong coverage includes: explicitly articulates AI hub aspiration; proposes strategies to attract AI investment, talent, or research institutions; frames the jurisdiction as a future AI center or innovation hub.

label: Sr Civil Society Capacity
description: Civil society capacity: building NGO ability to engage AI governance

label: Sr Digital Leapfrogging
description: The extent to which the statement frames AI adoption as an opportunity to skip traditional development stages. Strong coverage includes: explicitly invokes digital leapfrogging; frames AI as enabling countries to bypass intermediate development stages; proposes AI-first strategies for healthcare, education, or infrastructure in developing contexts.

label: Sr Digital Literacy Emphasis
description: Digital literacy emphasis: training public to understand AI

label: Sr Government Capacity
description: Government capacity: building state ability to govern AI

label: Sr International Capacity Gap
description: International capacity gap: addressing global inequality in AI governance ability

label: Sr Private Sector Self Regulation
description: Private sector self-regulation: industry-led standards and codes

label: Sr Public Ai Investment
description: Public AI investment: government funding for AI development

label: Sr Public Trust Building
description: Public trust building: initiatives to increase AI confidence

label: Sr Regulatory Expertise
description: Regulatory expertise: specialized technical capacity for regulators

label: Sr Research Infrastructure
description: Research infrastructure: computing, data, facilities for AI research

label: Sr State As Platform
description: The extent to which the statement envisions government providing AI infrastructure, data platforms, or computational resources for private sector use. Strong coverage includes: explicitly proposes state-as-platform model; government provides AI infrastructure (compute, data, APIs) for private sector and civil society use; frames public AI infrastructure as public good.

label: Sr Testbed Sandbox Infrastructure
description: Testbed and sandbox infrastructure: facilities for safe AI experimentation

label: St Academic Research Freedom
description: Academic research freedom: independence of AI research

label: St Affected Community Standing
description: Affected community standing: formal role for impacted groups

label: St Big Tech Accountability
description: Big Tech accountability: obligations of major AI companies

label: St Child Protection Online
description: Child protection online: safeguards for minors in AI contexts

label: St Civil Society Role
description: Civil society role: NGOs, advocates in AI governance

label: St Consumer Protection
description: Consumer protection: rights of AI consumers

label: St Corporate Power Concentration
description: Corporate power concentration: concerns about Big Tech control

label: St Deployer Responsibilities
description: Deployer responsibilities: obligations of those who deploy AI

label: St Developer Responsibilities
description: Developer responsibilities: obligations of AI creators

label: St Developing Country Voice
description: Developing country voice: Global South in AI governance

label: St Elderly Vulnerable Protection
description: Elderly and vulnerable protection: special AI safeguards

label: St Government Role
description: Government role in AI: state responsibilities and powers

label: St Indigenous Data Sovereignty
description: Indigenous data sovereignty: First Nations control over AI and data

label: St Labor Voice
description: Labor voice: worker representation in AI governance

label: St Media Role
description: Media role: journalism, watchdog function in AI accountability

label: St Multistakeholder Governance
description: Multistakeholder governance: inclusive AI governance model

label: St Platform Liability
description: Platform liability: legal responsibility of AI/tech platforms

label: St Professional Ethics Codes
description: Professional ethics codes: domain-specific AI ethics for practitioners

label: St Professional Sector Adaptation
description: The extent to which the statement addresses how specific professions or sectors must adapt to AI integration. Strong coverage includes: centrally addresses sector-specific AI adaptation; proposes profession-specific guidelines, training requirements, or practice standards for AI use in specific fields (medicine, law, education, engineering).

label: St Regulator Independence
description: Regulator independence: autonomy of AI oversight bodies from industry

label: St Researcher Responsibility
description: Researcher responsibility: ethical obligations of AI scientists

label: St Small State Capacity
description: Small state capacity: AI governance for resource-constrained countries

label: St User Rights
description: User rights: entitlements of people using AI systems

label: Ts Alignment Research
description: AI alignment research: technical work on aligning AI goals with human values

label: Ts Capability Control
description: Capability control: limiting what AI systems can do

label: Ts Constitutional Ai
description: Constitutional AI: rule-based constraints on AI behavior

label: Ts Containment Sandboxing
description: Containment and sandboxing: isolating AI systems from real-world impact

label: Ts Deployment Gating
description: Deployment gating: staged rollout with safety checks

label: Ts Fail Safe Design
description: Fail-safe design: defaulting to safe behavior on failure

label: Ts Formal Verification
description: Formal verification: mathematical proofs of AI system properties

label: Ts Goal Stability
description: Goal stability: ensuring AI pursues intended objectives over time

label: Ts Human Override
description: Human override: ability to supersede AI decisions

label: Ts Incident Response
description: Incident response: procedures for AI safety incidents

label: Ts Interpretability Safety
description: Interpretability for safety: understanding models to prevent harms

label: Ts Model Evaluation Safety
description: Safety-focused model evaluation: benchmarks for safety properties

label: Ts Monitoring Anomaly Detection
description: Monitoring and anomaly detection: runtime safety monitoring

label: Ts Red Teaming Safety
description: Red teaming for safety: adversarial testing for safety failures

label: Ts Reward Hacking Prevention
description: Reward hacking prevention: stopping AI from gaming evaluation metrics

label: Ts Robustness Adversarial
description: Adversarial robustness: resistance to attacks and distribution shift

label: Ts Scalable Oversight
description: Scalable oversight: supervising AI that exceeds human capabilities

label: Ts Shutdown Mechanisms
description: Shutdown mechanisms: ability to safely stop AI systems

label: Ts Transparency Logging
description: Transparency logging: audit trails of AI system actions

label: Ts Value Learning
description: Value learning: AI learning human preferences and values

label: Vt Communitarian Framing
description: Communitarian framing: community and collective values

label: Vt Cosmopolitan Framing
description: Cosmopolitan framing: global citizenship and universal values

label: Vt Decolonial Framing
description: Decolonial framing: AI through postcolonial lens

description: Economic competitiveness framing: AI race dynamics

label: Vt Feminist Framing
description: Feminist framing: AI through gender equality lens

label: Vt Governance First Framing
description: Governance-first framing: institutional capacity before deployment

label: Vt Human Flourishing Framing
description: Human flourishing framing: AI for authentic human wellbeing

description: Innovation-first framing: prioritizing growth over precaution

label: Vt Libertarian Framing
description: Libertarian framing: minimal regulation, individual freedom

label: Vt National Security Framing
description: National security framing: AI through defense and security lens

label: Vt Precautionary Framing
description: Precautionary framing: better safe than sorry approach to AI

label: Vt Religious Moral Framing
description: Religious and moral framing: theological or traditional ethics

label: Vt Rights Based Framing
description: Rights-based framing: AI through human rights lens

label: Vt Risk Based Framing
description: Risk-based framing: proportionate response to AI risk levels

label: Vt Safety First Framing
description: Safety-first framing: prioritizing harm prevention over innovation

label: Vt Solidarity Framing
description: Solidarity framing: collective responsibility and mutual aid

description: Techno-optimism: positive framing of AI potential and progress
